ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 220, PLAINTIFFS BRIAN ROBERTS, GLENDA ROBERTS, DEIDRA ROE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS AMENDED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT CRANE CO. FAILED TO SUPPLY COMPLETE AND RESPONSIVE ANSWERS. 'WHERE THE QUESTION IS SPECIFIC AND EXPLICIT, AN ANSWER THAT SUPPLIES ONLY A PORTION OF THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IS WHOLLY INSUFFICIENT ['] ALSO, A PARTY MAY NOT DELIBERATELY MISCONSTRUE A QUESTION FOR THE PURPOSE OF SUPPLYING AN EVASIVE ANSWER, ['] ANSWERS MUST BE COMPLETE AND RESPONSIVE.' DEYO V. KILBOURNE (1978) 84 CAL.APP.3D 771, 783-784. DEFENDANT FAILED TO IDENTIFY AND SUMMARIZE THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENTS THAT FORMED THE BASIS OF ITS RESPONSES. DEFENDANT ALSO IMPERMISSIBLY RE-DEFINED INTERROGATORIES TO LIMIT THE SCOPE OF ITS RESPONSES TO 'PRODUCTS IDENTIFIED BY PLAINTIFFS.' DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS ABOUT THE TIMING OF DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS LACK MERIT. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE FULL RESPONSES WITHIN 20 DAYS. PLAINTIFFS' SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN CHAMBERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Apply Texas Law
Off Calendar Feb-14-2011 Continued To Mar-02-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Judin, Esq., of DEHAY ELLISTON (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Richard S. Chon, Esq., of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7003), appearing for defendant / joinder Georgia-Pacific LLC
Kevin M. Loew, Esq., of WATERS KRAUSE & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Brian Roberts, et al.
Joseph B. Adams, Esq., of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant J. T. Thorpe & Sons, Inc.

Ingrid Evans, Esq. and Jennifer McIntosh, Esq. of WATERS KRAUSE & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Brian Roberts, et al.
Nicole E. Gage, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant / opposing party Rockwell Automation, Inc.

RULING:
Plaintiffs Brian Roberts, et al.'s motion to compel further responses to Plaintiffs Request For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents And Tangible Things, Set One From Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc. Aruged and tentative ruling adopted except for requests 15 and 16. As to 15 and 16, defendants must identify all persons who were its PMKs in asbestos personal injury actions since 8/1/07 by name of PMK, case name and number and date of depo. Counsel to confer re place of production of documents and, if cannot agree, to seek a ruling per ex parte application.
Plaintiff's remaining motion is granted in part and denied in part and the request for sanctions is denied. The motion is granted as to requests 13-14, 17-19, 22-29, 33-38 and 42-45 because the documents sought are described with reasonable particularity and defendant's objections are without merit. The motion is denied as to requests 1-12 and 20-21 because those requests do not describe the documents sought with reasonable particularity. Plaintiff's separate statement omits any mention of requests 30-32 and 39-41. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied becasue many of their requests did not describe the documents sought with reasonable particularity. Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One From Defendant Crane Co. And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sundeep R. Patel, Esq. of WATERS KRAUS & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties BRIAN ROBERTS, GLENDA ROBERTS, DEIDRA ROE
Michele C. Barnes, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-249-1011), appearing for defendant / opposing party Crane Co.

RULING:
Plaintiffs BRIAN ROBERTS, GLENDA ROBERTS, DEIDRA ROE's motion to compel further responses to plaintiffs' special interrogatories, set one, and request for monetary sanctions. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion is granted. Defendant Crane Co. failed to supply complete and responsive answers. 'Where the question is specific and explicit, an answer that supplies only a portion of the information sought is wholly insufficient ['] Also, a party may not deliberately misconstrue a question for the purpose of supplying an evasive answer, ['] Answers must be complete and responsive.' Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 783-784. Defendant failed to identify and summarize the contents of the documents that formed the basis of its responses. Defendant also impermissibly re-defined interrogatories to limit the scope of its responses to 'products identified by plaintiffs.' Defendant's arguments about the timing of discovery obligations lack merit. Defendant to provide full responses within 20 days. Plaintiffs' sanctions is denied. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported = (220/HEK)

MOTION To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' Unverified First Amended Complaint For Damages
Off Calendar Jan-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Rachael Buckman, Esq. of CONNER & BISHOP (415-434-3006), appearing for defendant / moving party Fryer-Knowles, Inc.
Laurel Halbany, Esq. of WATERS KRAUS PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Brian Roberts

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRYER-KNOWLES, INC.'s motion to strike punitive damages. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Motion is denied. Plaintiffs' complaint sufficiently alleges a claim for punitive damages and sets forth the facts upon which the claim is premised. Plaintiffs allege that Fryer-Knowles has known of the health hazards of asbestos since at least the 1930s and concealed information regarding these hazards from the general public. Plaintiffs specifically allege that Fryer-Knowles suppressed the 'Lanza Report' and influenced the author to change his report, thereby concealing information from Plaintiff and other users. Plaintiffs further allege that Fryer-Knowles's officers, directors, and managing agents had knowledge of and authorized the allegedly fraudulent, oppressive, and malicious acts. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR #12365
